meaning: n.动摇,开始退让,踌躇,挥动者vi.摇摆,颤抖,摆动,摇曳,犹豫
examples: He felt sorry for him and began first to waver and then to weaken.
He began to waver in his resolution when a patrol wagon rolled up and more officers dismounted.
As a result, their discipline can waver and it can lead to dismal returns.
Dave is an E-waver who also spends a lot of time studying breadth characteristics.
